info.name: BEEAH Broker Backend
info.description: NestJS backend API for the BEEAH Broker Portal (beeah-broker-backend). Covers auth, users, leads, EOI, bookings, commissions, dashboard, payment plans, projects, sales bookings, sales offers, units, documents, and agency onboarding (create-new-account).

request.description: Registers a new user, issues auth tokens, and warms the Salesforce session.
Authenticates a user by email/password and issues auth tokens.
Issues a new access/refresh token pair from a valid refresh token (sent as the Bearer token).
Logs the current user out by clearing their stored refresh token.
Returns the currently authenticated user's profile.
Creates a new Salesforce lead. The interested project is optional, matching the real Salesforce broker portal lead-creation form.
Fetches every lead owned by the authenticated Salesforce user, paginated.
Searches the authenticated user's leads by id, name, email, or mobile phone.

Fetches the fixed nationality list (label/value pairs, value is the demonym e.g. "Indian") for populating a select input. Matches Salesforce's Nationality__c restricted picklist exactly. Public endpoint (no auth) - needed on the onboarding form before the user is logged in.

Fetches the fixed country list (label/value pairs, both the country name e.g. "Austria") for populating a select input. Matches Salesforce's Country/CountryOfResidence restricted picklist exactly. Public endpoint (no auth) - needed on the onboarding form before the user is logged in.

Creates the EOI only - documents (POST /documents/upload) and mode-of-payment (POST /eoi/mode-of-payment/:eoiId) are separate follow-up calls against the recordId this returns, not bundled into this request. Company doesn't collect its own applicant identity in the app UI - the required top-level firstName/lastName/email/countryCode/mobileNo are derived from the signatory (Representative_First_Name split, Signatory_Email, Signatory_Mobile/Signatory_Mobile_Country_Code), and country/countryOfResidence/nationality default to United Arab Emirates. This request's test script auto-captures the response recordId into the {{recordId}}/{{eoiId}} collection variables for the Upload Document / Create Mode of Payment requests below.

body.raw:
{
  "projectId": "{{projectId}}",
  "buyerType": "Company",
  "firstName": "Ahmed",
  "lastName": "Al Mansoori",
  "email": "ahmed.almansoori@alnoorrealestate.ae",
  "countryCode": "United Arab Emirates: 971",
  "mobileNo": 509876543,
  "countryOfResidence": "United Arab Emirates",
  "country": "United Arab Emirates",
  "firstApplicantAddress": "Dubai",
  "city": "",
  "leadSource": "Broker",
  "recordTypeDeveloperName": "Company",
  "nationality": "United Arab Emirates",
  "createdByPortalUser": true,
  "companyName": "Al Noor Real Estate Brokerage LLC",
  "companyRegistrationPlace": "Dubai",
  "companyRegistrationDate": "2015-01-01",
  "tradeLicenseNumber": "TL-123456",
  "tradeLicenseExpiryDate": "2030-01-01",
  "mobileCountryCode": "United Arab Emirates: 971",
  "companyEmail": "info@alnoorrealestate.ae",
  "vatCertificateNo": "VAT-100234567800003",
  "representativeFirstName": "Ahmed Al Mansoori",
  "signatoryMobile": "509876543",
  "signatoryMobileCountryCode": "United Arab Emirates: 971",
  "signatoryEmail": "ahmed.almansoori@alnoorrealestate.ae",
  "unitPreferences": [
    {
      "unitType": "studio",
      "noOfUnits": 1,
      "eoiAmount": 15000
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Same endpoint as "Create EOI - Company", buyerType "Individual" with Residency Type = UAE - collects the applicant's own identity/address fields plus Emirates ID (eidNo/emiratesExpiry), no passportExpiry. None of the Company_*/Representative_*/Signatory_* fields are sent.

body.raw:
{
  "projectId": "{{projectId}}",
  "buyerType": "Individual",
  "firstName": "Ahmed",
  "lastName": "Khan",
  "email": "ahmed.khan@example.com",
  "countryCode": "United Arab Emirates: 971",
  "mobileNo": 501234567,
  "countryOfResidence": "United Arab Emirates",
  "country": "United Arab Emirates",
  "city": "Abu Dhabi",
  "leadSource": "Broker",
  "recordTypeDeveloperName": "Individual",
  "nationality": "United Arab Emirates",
  "eidNo": "784199012345671",
  "emiratesExpiry": "2030-01-01",
  "firstApplicantAddress": "123 Sample Street, Downtown",
  "postalCode": "12345",

  "unitPreferences": [
    {
      "unitType": "studio",

}

request.description: Same endpoint as "Create EOI - Company", buyerType "Individual" with Residency Type = NON UAE - collects passportExpiry instead of Emirates ID (eidNo/emiratesExpiry omitted).
Fetches every EOI owned by the authenticated Salesforce user, paginated.
Records one or more mode-of-payment (Bank Transfer/Cheque/Cash) entries against an EOI.

request.description: Fetches every booking record, paginated.
Fetches a single booking by id.
Creates a new booking.
Updates an existing booking.
Deletes a booking by id.
Fetches every commission record, paginated.
Fetches a single commission by id.
Creates a new commission.
Updates an existing commission.
Deletes a commission by id.
Fetches lead/EOI/booking status counts for the authenticated Salesforce user.
Fetches the payment plan for the given Salesforce building.
Fetches every project record available in Salesforce, paginated.
Searches projects whose name contains the given text, paginated.
Fetches a single project by its Salesforce project id.
Fetches every sales booking owned by the authenticated Salesforce user, paginated.
Creates a new sales offer for the given opportunity.
Fetches every unit belonging to the given project, paginated.
Searches units within a project whose name or code contains the given text (case-insensitive), paginated.
Fetches the unit preference (unit type) options and token amount for a given project.

Fetches a single unit's details by id within the given project. There's no dedicated Salesforce get-unit-by-id call, so this fetches every unit for the project (same as get-all) and returns the one matching unitId.
Uploads a base64-encoded document against a Salesforce record. Common endpoint shared by any flow that needs to attach a document.
Fetches a previously uploaded document's base64 content, for previewing.

Uploads a single onboarding document (individual or company) before the final `add` submission. No recordId - no Salesforce record exists yet. Returns a documentId to reference in that submission's `documents` array (e.g. `{"documents": [{"documentId": "<this response's documentId>"}]}`). Call this once per document. Public endpoint (no auth) as part of the onboarding flow.

Submits a new company agency onboarding application (companyInformation + signatoryDetails + optional licensePartners/managers/employees + bankInfo + documents). Public endpoint (no auth) as part of the onboarding flow. Exactly one of companyInformation/personalDetails must be present. Note: companyInformation.country is a free-text Salesforce field (send the country name), but signatoryDetails.authorizedSignatoryCountryCode and signatoryDetails.nationality map to restricted picklists - same "Country: dialCode" and demonym rules as the individual endpoint. `documents` only references documentIds - upload each file first via POST /create-new-account/upload-document. NOTE: as of this writing, Salesforce rejects "Emirati" here with INVALID_OR_NULL_FOR_RESTRICTED_PICKLIST even though the individual endpoint's equivalent field accepts demonyms fine - see the 'Nationality Diagnostic' request below.

item.name: Create New Account - Company (Nationality Diagnostic - Indian)

request.description: DIAGNOSTIC - identical to 'Create New Account - Company (UAE/International Agency)' except signatoryDetails/licensePartners/managers.nationality is "Indian" instead of "Emirati". Salesforce rejected "Emirati" with INVALID_OR_NULL_FOR_RESTRICTED_PICKLIST on Nationality__c, even though "Indian" is confirmed working on the individual endpoint's equivalent field. Run this to isolate whether "Emirati" specifically is missing from this field's configured picklist values (this succeeds) or whether the whole demonym approach is wrong for the company-side fields (this also fails).

request.description: Submits a new individual (self-licensed) agency onboarding application (personalDetails + bankInfo + optional licensePartners + documents). Public endpoint (no auth) as part of the onboarding flow. Exactly one of onboarding/personalDetails must be present. Note: personalDetails.countryCode and personalDetails.nationality map to Salesforce restricted picklists - countryCode must be "Country: dialCode" (e.g. "United Arab Emirates: 971", not "971"), and nationality must be the demonym (e.g. "Indian", not "India"). `documents` only references documentIds - upload each file first via POST /create-new-account/upload-document.

body.raw:
{
  "personalDetails": {
    "type": "Individual",
    "subType": "Self-Licensed Residence",
    "firstName": "Aanand",
    "lastName": "Yadav",
    "nationality": "Indian",
    "passportNumber": "P123456789",
    "passportExpiry": "2029-09-09",
    "emiratesId": "784-1989-1234567-1",
    "eidExpiry": "2029-09-09",
    "countryCode": "United Arab Emirates: 971",
    "mobile": "501234567",
    "email": "agent@example.com",
    "addressLine1": "Dubai",
    "addressLine2": "",
    "country": "United Arab Emirates",
    "city": "Dubai",
    "poBox": "456789765",
    "srerdNumber": "89757467890",
    "brokerCardDetails": "RERA",

  },
  "bankInfo": {

    "bankAccountNumber": "1234567890",

      "nationality": "Indian",
      "emiratesId": "784-1989-1234567-1",
      "passportNo": "P123456789",
      "role": "Share Owner",
      "sharePercentage": 100
    }
  ],
  "documents": [
    {
      "documentId": "a0BO100000HLHiPMAX"
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Fetches the mandatory document checklist for a given agency sub-type. Public endpoint (no auth) as part of the onboarding flow.
Fetches the list of valid agency sub-types, for populating the onboarding form's subType dropdown. Public endpoint (no auth) as part of the onboarding flow.
